title: santiria griffithii
intro: Santiria griffithii, also known as the Wild Camia or Kassod tree, is a plant species native to Malaysia and other parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Burseraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Santiria griffithii include reducing fever, alleviating cough and cold symptoms, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Santiria griffithii are often made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
